# OTP Insight — 2026-06-19

*Generated by the OTP Dreamer (Phase 0: manual dreaming pass). Read-only proposal — nothing in
the pipeline was changed. Window: 1 service day, 155,055 trip-stop observations, 6,222 trips.*

## Headline
**System OTP 55.7%** (on-time = −1..+5 min) · avg +2.6 min · median +1.5 min.

## 🔑 Key finding: "low OTP" hides *two opposite* failure modes
The worst-OTP routes split cleanly into **chronically late** and **chronically early** — and a
single OTP % conflates them. This is the most actionable pattern in the data:

**Chronically LATE (real reliability problem):**
| Route | OTP | median late |
|---|---|---|
| 236 (South Shore Plaza–Quincy) | 25.1% | **+9.0 min** |
| 350 (N Burlington–Alewife) | 30.9% | +4.1 |
| 240 (Avon Sq–Ashmont) | 30.6% | +6.5 |
| 132, 553, CR-Fitchburg | 34–36% | +5–6 |

**Chronically EARLY (different problem — riders miss departures):**
| Route | OTP | median | note |
|---|---|---|---|
| 8 (Harbor Pt–Kenmore) | 24.5% | **−4.1** | 884/1267 obs early |
| Green-E | 33.4% | −2.1 | light rail |
| 23 (Ashmont–Ruggles) | 35.7% | −1.8 | 1593 obs early |
| Mattapan | 33.9% | −1.6 | 0 late |

→ **Proposed mart:** split OTP into **late-breach %** vs **early-breach %** (don't average them away).

## 🗺️ Delays concentrate by *corridor / mode*
- **Green Line light-rail branches run systematically EARLY** — E-branch (Northeastern, MFA,
  Brigham Circle, Longwood, Prudential) all ~0–6% OTP at **−4 to −6 min**; the new GLX Medford
  branch (Medford/Tufts, Ball Sq, Magoun Sq, Gilman Sq) even earlier at **−7 to −8 min**.
- **The Bridge St bus corridor runs chronically LATE** — Bridge St @ Princeton/Birchbrow/Evans/
  Standish/Fairlawn all ~0–5% OTP at **+10–11 min**. A clear lateness hotspot.
→ **Proposed mart:** corridor/branch + **mode (light-rail / bus / commuter-rail)** rollups —
  light rail skews early, buses skew late.

## ⏰ OTP degrades through the day
Best in the AM (**06:00 67% · 07:00 63%**), declines through midday to a low (**13:00 47%**),
partial PM recovery. Worth a system-level hourly trend tile.

## ⚠️ Data-quality caveat (adversarial-verify caught this)
The hour-08 dip (38.7%) and hour-09 (0%) sit on tiny observation counts (3,062 and 43 vs ~20k
elsewhere) — **capture-window artifacts, not real transit patterns** (the poller wasn't running
pre-~10:46 today). Excluded from the conclusions above. *This is the verifier doing its job:
not every anomaly is real.*

## Proposed next marts (for human review — not auto-built)
1. `gold.breach_by_route` — separate early-breach vs late-breach rates.
2. `gold.otp_by_mode` — light-rail / bus / commuter-rail.
3. `gold.otp_by_corridor` — branch/corridor rollup (surfaces Green-early vs Bridge-St-late).
4. System hourly OTP trend (already derivable from `otp_by_route_hour`).
